concept: Counterexample / Case That Defeats a General Claim
description: A case that falls within a general claim's own stated scope and lacks the property the claim says every such case has. A genuine counterexample refutes the claim as stated; a case outside the scope, or one that merely surprises, does not.
sound_symbolism: The breath and bright vowel of 'phi' recall the beginning of 'phisuwa' (example), a kinship the ear can catch at once. Under stress, 'whe' changes that breath into a rounded voiceless glide and widens the middle of the word. A sharp velar contact in 'ki' releases into focused 'i', bringing the familiar opening through resistance to one precise point.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha phiwheki ra sherewa whekate.' (This counterexample refutes that claim.) The targeted claim may modify the noun possessively: 'sherewa phiwheki' (a counterexample to the claim). Distinct from an ordinary 'phisuwa' (example) and from any case that merely differs from what was expected.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A single encountered case can loosen attachment to an overgeneralized view and invite a more conditional understanding.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Counterexamples let participants challenge universality through a case rather than through force, status, or personal dismissal.

tags.cognition: a scoped case that disproves a general claim as stated
tags.dialogue: a concrete way to challenge overgeneralization
tags.wisdom: attention to particulars that resist an overly broad rule
